title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s signal is unusually concentrated in evaluation and assurance limits: new papers probe whether current attack benchmarks, behavioral tests, and deployment checks actually cover the risks they claim to measure. Alongside that, fresh work highlights quantization and backdoor attack surfaces, while OpenAI and Partnership on AI published operational updates on sensitive-conversation safeguards and practical risk management.

items.title: Taxonomy and coverage audit maps gaps in LLM attack benchmarks
items.why_it_matters: Offers a structured way to see which inference-time attack classes current benchmarks miss.

items.title: Behavioral assurance cannot verify all safety claims governance now expects, paper argues
items.why_it_matters: Challenges whether current eval-based assurance is sufficient for regulatory safety evidence.

items.title: Training ML models with predictable failures targets deployment-scale risk estimation
items.why_it_matters: Addresses how to estimate rare but consequential failures before large-scale deployment.

items.title: Quantization-permanent unlearning aims to stop deleted knowledge from reappearing after compression
items.why_it_matters: Highlights that unlearning claims may fail after standard deployment-time quantization.

items.title: Outlier injection attack widens quantization-induced malicious behavior in LLMs
items.why_it_matters: Shows model compression can create a practical security gap between audited and deployed behavior.

items.title: MetaBackdoor identifies positional encoding as a new LLM backdoor attack surface
items.why_it_matters: Expands the set of components defenders must inspect beyond content-triggered backdoors.

items.title: OpenAI updates ChatGPT to better recognize context in sensitive conversations
items.why_it_matters: Signals a shift toward longitudinal risk detection rather than single-turn safety checks.

items.title: OpenAI details response to TanStack npm supply chain attack
items.why_it_matters: A concrete incident report on AI vendor software supply-chain security and remediation.

items.title: Partnership on AI calls for moving from theory to action in AI risk management
items.why_it_matters: Reflects continued pressure to operationalize governance and risk practices beyond principles.

items.title: RoSHAP proposes a robustness-focused metric for stable feature attribution
items.why_it_matters: Improves interpretability reliability by measuring whether explanations remain stable across variation.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
